title: Compressed Air Leak Cost Calculator
description: Industrial-grade tool to quantify energy and financial losses from compressed air leaks, based on ISO 50001, Lean Six Sigma, and WERC guidelines.

inputs.label: System Pressure
inputs.businessContext: Operating pressure of the compressed air system; higher pressure increases leak flow rate.

businessContext_i18n.en: Operating pressure of the compressed air system; higher pressure increases leak flow rate.

inputs.label: Equivalent Leak Diameter
inputs.businessContext: Diameter of a round orifice representing the average leak size. Common sizes: 1/8", 1/4", 1/2".

businessContext_i18n.en: Diameter of a round orifice representing the average leak size. Common sizes: 1/8", 1/4", 1/2".

inputs.label: Number of Leaks
inputs.businessContext: Total number of identified or estimated leaks in the system.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total number of identified or estimated leaks in the system.

inputs.label: Annual Operating Hours
inputs.businessContext: Total hours the compressor runs per year. Continuous operation = 8760 h.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total hours the compressor runs per year. Continuous operation = 8760 h.

inputs.label: Electricity Cost
inputs.businessContext: Average blended cost of electricity per kilowatt-hour.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average blended cost of electricity per kilowatt-hour.

inputs.label: Compressor Efficiency
inputs.businessContext: Overall efficiency of the compressor system (motor + drive + compressor). Typical range 70-85%.

businessContext_i18n.en: Overall efficiency of the compressor system (motor + drive + compressor). Typical range 70-85%.

inputs.label: Leak Type
inputs.businessContext: Geometry of the leak affects discharge coefficient. Round orifice is most common.

businessContext_i18n.en: Geometry of the leak affects discharge coefficient. Round orifice is most common.

inputs.label: Include Maintenance Cost
inputs.businessContext: If true, adds estimated labor and material cost for leak repair.

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, adds estimated labor and material cost for leak repair.

rules.message: System pressure must be between 60 and 200 psig.
Leak diameter must be between 0.01 and 1.0 inches.
Compressor efficiency must be between 50% and 95%.

thresholds.message: Annual leak cost exceeds $50,000. Immediate action recommended.
Compressor efficiency below 70%. Consider maintenance or upgrade.
Leak diameter > 0.25 inches indicates large leaks. Prioritize repair.

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
